title: Height Study Seminar
content:
I’m organizing a study seminar on the theory of heights, based on the book Heights in Diophantine Geometry.
We will be focusing on three results: Mordell-Weil theorem, Falting theorem, and potentially Vojta conjecture. If time permits, we will also try to cover Manin’s conjecture for toric varieties. We will start slow and spend one or two talks on naive heights on projective space.  Then we will define Weil heights for projective varieties, and study their properties. After this, we will focus on abelian varieties, and once we are familar with those objects, we introduce Neron-Tate heights, and finally prove the first main result we want to cover (actually, I think this will be all we can do this term (spring 2024)).
Talk 1: Introduction to Naive Heights (2024-05-20)In this talk, we will review some number theory, then present the definition of heights for projective space.

title: About Me
content:
Who I am, what I do and contact. 

Who Am I?I’m a PhD student in University of Waterloo, working in algebraic geometry, under the supervision of Prof. Matthew Satriano.
Outside of School, I enjoy cats, video games, music and horror films. 
What I do?Here is a short list of things I’m trying to think at the back of my head:

Heights on toric stacks
Toric vector bundles
Hilbert schemes

Contact?Emaild9dai at uwaterloo dot ca

title: MATH 146 Materials, Winter 2023
content:
In Winter 2023, I was one of the teaching assistant for MATH 146, linear algebra 1. 
Here contains some notes and slides I prepared for the tutorials. In particular, for the hangouts, exercises are included. 

 In the slides, you will see my deadname, please just ignore!

Why Linear AlgebraIn this slide, I tried to motivate the notion of linear transformation by trying to find a general formula for Fibonacci sequence. 
Click to download
Combinatorial DesignIn this handout, congruent matrices are introduced to the students, via the mean of a problem from combinatorial design.
Click to download
The appendix
Permutation MatricesIn this two tutorial, I defined permutations and permutation matrices associated with them. 
Permutations:Click to download
Permutation matrices:Click to download
DeterminantsIn this tutorial, I provided an alternative definition of determinants, based on the permutations (in the course we defined determinants recursively using row/column expansion). 
Click to download
Linear CodesHere I explained how to use linear algebra to encode messages and detect errors.
Click to download
Linear RecursionIn the slides, I tried to explore the seemingly innocent question of when would a linear recurssion eventually be positive. 
Click to download
